title: Simple 24" Bath Vanity
summary: A 24"-wide plywood bathroom vanity cabinet with a toe kick, a decorative front apron, and full-overlay doors, sized for a standard vanity top.
description:
Cutting the plywood with the grain, longest cuts first, is what keeps this manageable as a one-person job: rip an 18"-wide strip off the sheet first, then cut the sides and shelf from that strip, rather than trying to lay out every piece across the full sheet at once. Most lumber yards will make these long rip cuts for you, which is worth asking for — it turns a full sheet of plywood into pieces that actually fit in a car.

The front apron gets a 4" gap left above it on purpose, sized to clear the vanity top's front edge and any faucet or fixture reach — skip that gap and the doors won't clear the top when installed. The decorative trim on the apron and doors is genuinely optional; the cabinet is fully functional without it, and it's really a styling choice for how finished the front reads.

materials.name: Pine, 1x6, 4 ft
materials.note: Optional toe-kick trim — 3/4" plywood works instead.

materials.name: Decorative trim stock, 1x2 x 1/4" thick, 8 ft
Pocket hole screws or countersunk screws/finish nails

cutList.note: Cut to fit each door face and the front apron.

steps.title: Cut the plywood
steps.body: Cut with the grain, longest cuts first — rip an 18"-wide strip off the sheet, then cut the sides and bottom shelf from that strip. Consider having the lumber yard make these long cuts for easier transport.

steps.title: Attach the bottom shelf
steps.body: Place the bottom shelf between the sides and attach with a pocket hole jig, or countersunk screws or 2" finish nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the face rails
steps.body: Attach the front and back face rails. The front rail needs a 4" space left above it to clear the vanity top and any fixtures. The back rails go at the top and bottom of the bottom shelf, centered.

steps.title: Cut and attach the toe kick
steps.body: Cut out the toe kick opening and attach with 2" finish nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Add the front apron trim
steps.body: Glue and attach the decorative trim pieces to the front apron, if using them.
materials: Decorative trim stock, 1x2 x 1/4" thick, 8 ft

steps.title: Trim and hang the doors
steps.body: Attach decorative trim to the door faces if desired, add edge banding to any stained edges, and hang the doors with full-overlay hinges.
materials: Decorative trim stock, 1x2 x 1/4" thick, 8 ft
